nonBlockingIssues.description: stop() does not reset container_id to None after killing the container. This means container_id() will still return Some(id) after stop() is called, which is misleading. Consider adding 'let mut guard = self.container_id.lock().unwrap(); *guard = None;' after the successful kill at line 464.

featureReviews.summary: Fix for tools-gvisor-support: container_id never stored after start(). The fix changes container_id from Option<String> to Mutex<Option<String>> for interior mutability, and properly stores the ID returned from docker run in start(). All dependent methods now correctly access the stored container_id via the mutex.
Fixed cache memory leak (expired entries now evicted on access) and get_lease_ttl() stub (now queries Vault's sys/leases/lookup API). All 11 vault tests pass.
Changed validation from requiring 'read' operation to accepting any non-empty allowed_operations. Git scopes (push/pull/fetch) and LLM scopes (chat/embed) now work correctly.

nonBlockingIssues.description: OpaRiskLevel enum is still defined but no longer used internally. It remains for API compatibility but OpaSubject::new() now accepts CedarRiskLevel. Consider whether to deprecate.
Breaking API change: OpaSubject::new() now takes CedarRiskLevel instead of OpaRiskLevel. If called from external code, would require updates. However appears internal-only.

featureReviews.summary: Consolidated duplicate ToolOperation enum by removing from opa_policy.rs and importing from cedar_policy.rs. Added compatibility methods to maintain OPA functionality.

Clarified FirecrackerSandbox is a STUB that does NOT provide actual microVM isolation. Renamed to FirecrackerSandboxStub, added deprecation warning, and comprehensive documentation about limitations and platform requirements.
